summary: The renamed navigation, labels, lifecycle actions, and hidden post-publish generation controls all behaved correctly through the live web UI.

assertions.details: The new `/persona-configs` route worked while the old `/persona-packs` route returned the app's not-found screen.
evidence: `http://localhost:5180/persona-packs` showed `Page not found` and `The route you requested is not defined in the Botchestra app shell yet.`

assertions.details: The sidebar label and navigation target were updated to `Persona Configurations`.
evidence: After signup, the sidebar displayed `Persona Configurations` next to Studies, Axis Library, and Transcripts.
Clicking the sidebar item navigated to `/persona-configs`.

assertions.details: User-visible text used `Persona Configuration` terminology on the list page, draft/detail pages, and study setup pages.
evidence: List page buttons/headers used `Create Persona Configuration` and `Persona Configurations`.
Detail pages used copy like `Review persona configuration metadata...` and `Publish persona configuration?`.
The study creation page showed `Persona configuration selector` and `Configure the persona configuration...`.

assertions.details: A real config was edited, given a synthetic user, published, and archived successfully after the rename.
evidence: The draft panel config showed `Draft changes saved.` after metadata edits and `Synthetic user added.` after adding a manual row.
Publishing changed the status to `published` / version `v2`, and archiving later changed the status to `archived` with `Persona configuration archived.`

assertions.details: Publish-time generation controls were hidden on non-draft configs.
evidence: On the published panel config, the page still showed the generation section but did not show `Generate Synthetic Users`, `Retry Failed`, or any `Regenerate` row buttons.
The annotated published screenshot exposed only `Archive` as the primary action.

assertions.details: Sidebar navigation across renamed areas worked as expected.
evidence: Using sidebar links moved the same session through `/studies/new`, `/axis-library`, `/transcripts`, and `/studies` successfully.
The study setup page also showed the renamed persona-configuration terminology.
